name: Stipe Miocic

qualityWinLedger.context: UFC heavyweight champion and elite title-level opponent.
Elite heavyweight title challenger and future champion.
Elite trilogy win, discounted from maximum credit for repeat-opponent, age, and final-fight context.
Former UFC champion and top heavyweight contender.
Former champion on relevant win streak.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Cormier I did not end it because Stipe recovered and won the trilogy. Ngannou II is endpoint. Jones late fight is post-prime.

lossContextEvents.context: Miocic recovered by winning the Cormier rematch and trilogy.
Late retirement fight after more than three years away.

shortCase: Stipe is the best UFC heavyweight resume case: the strongest heavyweight title-run argument, major wins over champions, and trilogy separation over Cormier.
bestArgument: Stipe's case starts with the best UFC heavyweight resume: title defenses, champion wins, the first Ngannou win, and the Cormier trilogy comeback.
counterArgument: The counterargument is division ceiling. Heavyweight is chaotic and thinner than lightweight or welterweight, so Stipe needs championship weight to carry the case.
whyRankedHere: Stipe lands here because he has the strongest UFC heavyweight resume: heavyweight title wins, major defenses, champion-level opponent quality, and the Cormier trilogy edge.
whyNotHigher: He does not rank higher because heavyweight depth and volatility cap the score compared with lighter divisions, and the loss column is real. The Jones loss also keeps the back end from feeling clean.
finalTakeaway: Stipe is the UFC heavyweight benchmark: the best heavyweight title resume, elite champion wins, and direct trilogy separation over Cormier, with heavyweight volatility holding him outside the top ten.
